use_context_daily: Safe for daily use as tea or smudge for dream enhancement and digestive support. Start with small amounts to assess sensitivity.
use_context_practitioner: Herbalists may recommend for menstrual regulation, digestive complaints, or as part of dream-work protocols.
use_context_ceremonial: Used in smudging ceremonies across European and Asian traditions. Burned to cleanse space and enhance visionary states.
use_context_group_vs_private: Both appropriate. Group smudging ceremonies are traditional; private dream pillows and teas are equally valid.
cultural_respect_notes: Widely used across cultures — European, Chinese (as Ai Ye in TCM), and Native American traditions. Acknowledge the tradition you are drawing from.
misuse_risks: Excessive internal use can cause liver stress due to thujone content. Not a substitute for medical care for menstrual disorders.
facilitator_qualifications: No special facilitation needed for basic tea or smudge use. Dream-work facilitation benefits from training in transpersonal psychology.
facilitator_qualities: Grounded presence, familiarity with dream symbolism, ability to hold space for unconscious material.
facilitator_red_flags: Anyone claiming mugwort will induce psychedelic-level experiences or cure serious illness.
preparation_framework: Set intention before sleep when using for dream work. Keep a dream journal nearby. Allow integration time in the morning.
physiological_contraindications: Avoid during pregnancy (emmenagogue). Caution with liver conditions. Allergy possible in those sensitive to Asteraceae family.
psychological_considerations: Dream enhancement may surface unconscious material. Those with PTSD or trauma history should approach dream work gradually.
environmental_considerations: Best used in quiet, safe sleep environments. Ensure good ventilation if burning.
dosage_sensitivity: Tea: 1-2 tsp dried herb per cup. Dream pillow: small sachet under pillow. Smudge: moderate smoke, well-ventilated space.
interaction_notes: May potentiate sedative herbs and medications. Avoid combining with blood-thinning medications.
native_ecosystems: Temperate regions of Europe, Asia, and North America. Thrives in disturbed soils, roadsides, and meadows.
wildcrafted_vs_cultivated: Abundantly available wild. Ethical wildcrafting is straightforward as it grows prolifically. Cultivation also easy.
sustainable_harvesting: Harvest aerial parts before flowering. Leave roots intact and at least half the stand for regeneration.
ethical_sourcing_concerns: Minimal concerns due to abundance. Ensure wildcrafted sources are free from pesticides and road pollution.
sourcing_standards: Organic preferred for internal use. Wildcrafted acceptable from clean environments.
traditional_preparation: Dried herb tea, dream pillows, smudge bundles, moxa sticks in TCM (moxibustion).
modern_preparation: Tinctures, capsules, essential oil (external only), tea blends.
preparation_potency_notes: Fresh herb is more aromatic; dried herb concentrates thujone. Smudge releases volatile oils for aromatic effect.
intentional_practices: Set a dream intention before sleep. Write it down. Place mugwort pillow or drink tea 30 minutes before bed.
psychospiritual_effects: Opens the dream gateway, enhances intuition, connects to lunar and feminine cycles of consciousness.
archetypal_resonance: The Dreamer, the Moon Priestess. Associated with Artemis/Diana — protector of liminal spaces.
nervous_system_influence: Mildly sedating and grounding. Calms the waking mind to allow dream consciousness to emerge.
consciousness_interaction: Thins the veil between waking and dreaming. Enhances dream recall and lucidity without altering waking consciousness.
spirit_teaching: Mugwort teaches us to trust the wisdom that comes in darkness and sleep — that not all knowing comes through the rational mind.
integration_body: Notice how your body feels upon waking. Record physical sensations that accompany dream content.
integration_heart: Allow emotional content from dreams to be felt without immediate analysis. Let feelings settle before interpreting.
integration_mind: Journal dream content immediately upon waking. Look for patterns over weeks, not single dreams.
integration_spirit: Dreams are one form of communication from the deeper self. Honor them as messages, not random noise.
healthy_integration_signs: Richer dream life, improved intuition, deeper self-understanding, feeling more connected to natural cycles.
incomplete_integration_signs: Obsessing over dream symbols, sleep disruption, anxiety about dream content, disconnection from waking life.
when_to_seek_support: If dreams become persistently disturbing, if sleep quality deteriorates, or if dream content triggers trauma responses.

use_context_daily: Gentle enough for regular use as tea or smoke. Traditionally used for relaxation, meditation enhancement, and mild euphoria.
use_context_practitioner: Herbalists may recommend for anxiety, insomnia, or as a meditation aid. Not widely used in clinical Western herbalism.
use_context_ceremonial: Sacred flower of ancient Egypt, used in temple ceremonies and spiritual practices for thousands of years.
use_context_group_vs_private: Both appropriate. Private meditation use is most common. Group tea ceremonies honor Egyptian tradition.
cultural_respect_notes: Deeply sacred in ancient Egyptian spirituality — associated with creation, rebirth, and the sun god Ra. Honor this lineage.
misuse_risks: Recreational overuse diminishes sacred relationship. Combining with alcohol or other substances increases risks.
facilitator_qualifications: No formal facilitation required for tea use. Meditation teachers can incorporate into guided practice.
facilitator_qualities: Reverence for the plant, understanding of its subtle effects, ability to guide meditation.
facilitator_red_flags: Marketing as a legal high, promoting excessive doses, combining with alcohol or drugs.
preparation_framework: Create quiet, contemplative space. Set intention for meditation or relaxation. Use in the evening hours.
physiological_contraindications: Avoid with MAOIs and blood pressure medications. Not recommended during pregnancy or breastfeeding.
psychological_considerations: Mildly psychoactive — may enhance emotional openness. Generally well-tolerated psychologically.
environmental_considerations: Quiet, comfortable setting ideal. Works well in meditation spaces, gardens, or calm home environments.
dosage_sensitivity: Tea: 5-10g dried flowers per cup. Tincture: 1-3ml. Smoke: small amounts. Effects are subtle at normal doses.
interaction_notes: Contains apomorphine and nuciferine — may interact with dopaminergic medications and blood pressure drugs.
native_ecosystems: Nile River region, East Africa, and parts of South Asia. Grows in shallow warm waters and ponds.
wildcrafted_vs_cultivated: Primarily cultivated for commercial use. Wild populations exist in native regions but are not commercially harvested.
sustainable_harvesting: Harvest flowers at peak bloom. Cultivated in ponds and water gardens. Sustainable when farmed responsibly.
ethical_sourcing_concerns: Quality varies greatly. Some products are adulterated or mislabeled. Source from reputable suppliers.
sourcing_standards: Whole dried flowers preferred over extracts. Organic cultivation ensures no chemical contamination.
traditional_preparation: Flowers steeped in wine (ancient Egyptian method), tea infusion, or dried and smoked.
modern_preparation: Tea, tincture, resin extract, vaporization of dried petals.
preparation_potency_notes: Wine extraction (traditional) may be more potent than water tea. Smoking provides more immediate but shorter effects.
intentional_practices: Sit quietly with the tea. Set an intention for inner peace or insight. Allow the subtle effects to unfold naturally.
psychospiritual_effects: Gentle euphoria, enhanced meditation, feelings of peace and connection. Opens the heart center subtly.
archetypal_resonance: The Sacred Lotus — symbol of spiritual awakening, creation emerging from primordial waters, divine beauty.
nervous_system_influence: Gently sedating and anxiolytic. Promotes parasympathetic activation without heavy sedation.
consciousness_interaction: Subtly shifts awareness toward meditative states. Enhances color perception and dream-like awareness while awake.
spirit_teaching: Blue Lotus teaches that the most profound openings are gentle ones — that spiritual awakening unfolds like a flower, not an explosion.
integration_body: Notice physical relaxation patterns. Allow the body to soften without forcing sleep.
integration_heart: Be present with whatever emotional softening arises. Blue Lotus opens gently — meet it with gentleness.
integration_mind: Allow thoughts to slow without grasping. Notice the quality of awareness rather than its content.
integration_spirit: Reflect on what emerges in the quiet space Blue Lotus creates. Journal insights from meditation.
healthy_integration_signs: Deeper meditation practice, improved relaxation response, enhanced dream recall, sense of inner peace.
incomplete_integration_signs: Dependence on the plant for relaxation, increasing doses for effect, using to avoid difficult emotions.
when_to_seek_support: If developing psychological dependence, if sleep is disrupted, or if experiencing unexpected mood changes.

use_context_daily: Not appropriate for daily use. Microdosing protocols (0.1-0.3g every 3 days) exist but lack long-term safety data.
use_context_practitioner: Clinical research settings using trained therapists. Legal psilocybin therapy emerging in Oregon and other jurisdictions.
use_context_ceremonial: Used in Mazatec velada ceremonies for millennia. Sacred sacrament requiring experienced curandero guidance.
use_context_group_vs_private: Ceremonial use is traditionally guided. Clinical use is one-on-one with therapist. Group retreat settings require experienced facilitators and proper screening.
cultural_respect_notes: Sacred medicine of the Mazatec people of Oaxaca. María Sabina lineage. Avoid appropriating indigenous ceremony while honoring the tradition.
misuse_risks: Recreational use without preparation or integration wastes therapeutic potential. Risk of psychological crisis without proper set and setting.
facilitator_qualifications: Licensed therapist with psychedelic-assisted therapy training, or traditional curandero with lineage. MAPS or equivalent certification preferred for clinical use.
facilitator_qualities: Deep personal experience, unshakeable calm, ability to be fully present without directing, trust in the process, ethical boundaries.
facilitator_red_flags: Sexual boundary violations, claiming to be a shaman without lineage, mixing substances, no screening process, no integration support.
preparation_framework: 2-4 weeks preparation: intention setting, dietary guidelines, psychological screening, establishing trust with facilitator. Post-session integration over weeks.
physiological_contraindications: Avoid with lithium (seizure risk). Caution with SSRIs (diminished effects or serotonin complications). Heart conditions require medical clearance.
psychological_considerations: Contraindicated in active psychosis, schizophrenia spectrum disorders, bipolar I with mania history. Trauma may surface — requires skilled support.
environmental_considerations: Safe physical environment essential. Nature settings or comfortable indoor spaces. No driving. Trusted sitter mandatory at higher doses.
dosage_sensitivity: Microdose: 0.1-0.3g. Low: 0.5-1g. Moderate: 1.5-3g. High: 3-5g. Heroic: 5g+. Species and individual sensitivity vary greatly.
interaction_notes: SSRIs reduce effects. Lithium is dangerous. MAOIs potentiate dramatically. Avoid alcohol. Cannabis may increase anxiety.
native_ecosystems: Found worldwide — Psilocybe cubensis in tropical/subtropical regions, P. semilanceata in temperate grasslands, P. azurescens in Pacific Northwest.
wildcrafted_vs_cultivated: Cultivated for research and therapeutic use. Wild foraging requires expert mycological identification — misidentification can be fatal.
sustainable_harvesting: Cultivated mushrooms are sustainable. Wild harvesting should leave mycelium intact. Never over-harvest wild patches.
ethical_sourcing_concerns: Legal status varies by jurisdiction. Support decriminalization and regulated access. Avoid exploitation of indigenous knowledge.
sourcing_standards: Lab-tested for potency and contaminants in clinical settings. Know your source and species for non-clinical use.
traditional_preparation: Eaten fresh or dried in Mazatec tradition, sometimes with cacao. Consumed in darkness with chanting (velada).
modern_preparation: Dried whole, powdered in capsules, lemon tek (lemon juice pre-digestion), tea, chocolate preparations.
preparation_potency_notes: Lemon tek may intensify and shorten experience. Tea is gentler on stomach. Potency varies by species, batch, and storage.
intentional_practices: Fast for 4-6 hours beforehand. Set clear intention. Create altar or sacred space. Prayer or meditation before ingestion.
psychospiritual_effects: Ego dissolution, mystical experiences, profound interconnectedness, emotional catharsis, life review, encounter with the numinous.
archetypal_resonance: The Cosmic Mirror — reflecting the totality of self. The Mycelial Web — interconnection of all life. Death-rebirth mysteries.
nervous_system_influence: Activates serotonin 5-HT2A receptors. Creates neural hyperconnectivity. Temporarily dissolves default mode network patterns.
consciousness_interaction: Dissolves habitual perceptual filters. Allows direct experience of consciousness itself. Can access transpersonal dimensions.
spirit_teaching: The mushroom teaches that separation is an illusion — that you are not apart from nature but a part of it. What you seek is already within.
integration_body: Rest the day after. Eat nourishing food. Gentle movement. Notice how the body holds or releases tension differently.
integration_heart: Allow all emotions that arose to be felt fully in the days following. Do not rush to normalize. Share with trusted others.
integration_mind: Journal insights within 24 hours. Revisit over weeks. Some meanings emerge slowly. Avoid premature conclusions.
integration_spirit: The experience points toward something — follow that thread in daily life. Let insights change behavior, not just beliefs.
healthy_integration_signs: Increased compassion, reduced anxiety, greater life satisfaction, improved relationships, sense of meaning and purpose.
incomplete_integration_signs: Spiritual bypassing, grandiosity, social withdrawal, chasing the next experience, inability to function normally.
when_to_seek_support: If experiencing persistent anxiety, depersonalization, psychotic symptoms, or inability to return to baseline after 2 weeks.

use_context_daily: Medical patients may use daily under practitioner guidance. Recreational daily use carries dependence risk. Tolerance develops quickly.
use_context_practitioner: Medical cannabis practitioners prescribe for chronic pain, nausea, seizures, PTSD, and other conditions with dosing protocols.
use_context_ceremonial: Used in Rastafari as sacrament, in Hindu tradition as bhang (Shiva association), and in some African spiritual practices.
use_context_group_vs_private: Both common. Private medicinal use is standard. Group ceremonial use exists in specific traditions.
cultural_respect_notes: Sacred plant in Rastafari and Hindu traditions. Used medicinally for 5000+ years across cultures. Honor these lineages.
misuse_risks: Psychological dependence, amotivational syndrome with chronic heavy use, cognitive impairment in developing brains (under 25).
facilitator_qualifications: Medical cannabis doctor for therapeutic use. No facilitation needed for personal use where legal.
facilitator_qualities: For therapeutic use: understanding of cannabinoid medicine, strain selection, dosing titration, and harm reduction.
facilitator_red_flags: Promoting heavy use as harmless, selling to minors, dismissing dependence concerns, ignoring mental health contraindications.
preparation_framework: Start low, go slow. Understand THC:CBD ratios. Keep a usage journal. Take regular tolerance breaks.
physiological_contraindications: Caution with cardiovascular conditions (increases heart rate). Avoid smoking with respiratory conditions. Not for pregnant/nursing individuals.
psychological_considerations: May exacerbate anxiety, paranoia, or psychosis in predisposed individuals. Adolescent use impacts brain development.
environmental_considerations: Safe setting important especially for new users or high doses. Do not drive or operate machinery.
dosage_sensitivity: Edibles: start at 2.5-5mg THC. Inhalation: one small puff, wait 10 minutes. Individual sensitivity varies enormously.
interaction_notes: May interact with blood thinners, sedatives, and some psychiatric medications. CYP450 enzyme interactions possible.
native_ecosystems: Central Asia (Hindu Kush region). Now cultivated worldwide in diverse climates.
wildcrafted_vs_cultivated: Almost exclusively cultivated. Wild hemp exists but is not used medicinally. Landrace strains preserved by seed banks.
sustainable_harvesting: Indoor cultivation is energy-intensive. Outdoor sun-grown is more sustainable. Regenerative cannabis farming emerging.
ethical_sourcing_concerns: War on drugs has caused massive social harm. Support equity programs. Consider social justice in purchasing.
sourcing_standards: Lab-tested for potency, pesticides, heavy metals, and mold. Licensed dispensaries in legal markets.
traditional_preparation: Bhang (milk drink in India), smoked in chillum, hashish pressing, infused oils and butters.
modern_preparation: Flower, concentrates, edibles, tinctures, topicals, vaporization, capsules, transdermal patches.
preparation_potency_notes: Edibles are delayed (1-2 hours) and longer-lasting. Concentrates are very potent. Start with flower for dose control.
intentional_practices: Set intention before use. Mindful consumption rather than habitual. Consider journaling or creative practice alongside.
psychospiritual_effects: Enhanced sensory awareness, creative flow, introspection, relaxation, altered time perception, connection to body.
archetypal_resonance: The Relaxer and Revealer — softens mental rigidity, reveals hidden patterns of thought and feeling.
nervous_system_influence: Complex — can both calm (CBD dominant) and stimulate anxiety (high THC). Activates endocannabinoid system.
consciousness_interaction: Shifts attention to present-moment sensory experience. Can enhance or diminish metacognition depending on dose and strain.
spirit_teaching: Cannabis teaches presence and sensory appreciation — but also reveals how easily we can use pleasure to avoid growth.
integration_body: Notice relationship between cannabis use and body awareness. Track sleep quality, appetite, and energy patterns.
integration_heart: Be honest about whether use opens or closes emotional processing. Notice avoidance patterns.
integration_mind: Track cognitive effects honestly. Is use enhancing creativity or substituting for it?
integration_spirit: Examine your relationship with the plant honestly. Is it a teacher or a habit? Regular breaks provide clarity.
healthy_integration_signs: Intentional use, maintained motivation, enhanced creativity or pain relief, ability to take breaks easily.
incomplete_integration_signs: Inability to stop, using to numb emotions, decreased motivation, social isolation, escalating use.
when_to_seek_support: If unable to reduce use despite wanting to, if experiencing paranoia or anxiety, if daily functioning is impaired.

use_context_daily: Not for daily use. Ceremonial medicine requiring significant preparation, fasting, and integration periods between sessions.
use_context_practitioner: Requires experienced facilitator — traditionally an ayahuascero/curandero with years of training and plant dieta.
use_context_ceremonial: Sacred medicine of Amazonian indigenous peoples. Used in nighttime ceremony with icaros (sacred songs) and trained guide.
use_context_group_vs_private: Traditionally group ceremony with individual attention. One-on-one sessions exist but group container is traditional.
cultural_respect_notes: Sacred medicine of the Shipibo, Ashaninka, and dozens of Amazonian peoples. Deeply rooted in indigenous cosmology. Appropriation is a serious concern — support indigenous-led ceremonies.
misuse_risks: Extremely dangerous without proper screening, preparation, and facilitation. Deaths have occurred in unregulated settings from medical contraindications and negligent facilitators.
facilitator_qualifications: Years of apprenticeship in Amazonian tradition, extensive personal dieta, knowledge of contraindications. Clinical settings require medical screening protocols.
facilitator_qualities: Deep humility, mastery of icaros, ability to navigate dark experiences, genuine lineage, sobriety, clear ethical boundaries.
facilitator_red_flags: Sexual misconduct, mixing traditions carelessly, no medical screening, excessive pricing, messianic claims, isolation tactics, no integration support.
preparation_framework: 2-4 week dietary preparation (no tyramine foods, no medications). Psychological screening. Intention setting. Post-ceremony integration over weeks/months.
physiological_contraindications: STRICT: SSRIs, MAOIs, SNRIs (serotonin syndrome risk — potentially fatal). Heart conditions. Liver disease. Epilepsy. Many medications are dangerous.
psychological_considerations: Contraindicated in psychotic disorders, severe PTSD without experienced trauma-informed facilitator, active suicidality. Can surface deep trauma.
environmental_considerations: Nighttime ceremony in safe container. Experienced helpers present. No sharp objects. Clear path to bathroom. Nature setting traditional.
dosage_sensitivity: Dosage determined by experienced facilitator based on brew strength and individual sensitivity. Never self-dose without experience.
interaction_notes: Contains MAOIs (harmine/harmaline) — contraindicated with SSRIs, tyramine foods, stimulants, and many medications. Potentially fatal interactions.
native_ecosystems: Amazon rainforest — Peru, Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador. The vine (B. caapi) and leaf (P. viridis) grow in tropical lowland forest.
wildcrafted_vs_cultivated: Both. Wild-harvested in traditional contexts. Increasingly cultivated due to demand. Vine takes years to mature.
sustainable_harvesting: Overharvesting is a real concern as tourism increases. Support reforestation projects. Vine takes 5-10 years to mature.
ethical_sourcing_concerns: Major concerns: exploitation of indigenous knowledge, ayahuasca tourism disrupting communities, overharvesting, unregulated retreat centers.
sourcing_standards: Support indigenous-led organizations. Verify retreat center ethics. Look for reciprocity programs that give back to source communities.
traditional_preparation: Bark of B. caapi vine and leaves of P. viridis boiled together for hours in ceremonial fashion by trained maker.
modern_preparation: Same traditional brewing. Some use analogues (pharmahuasca) which lack the traditional preparation relationship. Retreat center preparations vary.
preparation_potency_notes: Potency varies greatly by brew. Experienced brewers adjust strength. The vine is considered the teacher; the leaf provides the vision.
intentional_practices: Extended dieta (dietary and behavioral restrictions), prayer, intention setting, abstinence, psychological preparation with facilitator.
psychospiritual_effects: Profound visionary experiences, encounter with plant intelligence, emotional purging, life review, cosmic consciousness, ego dissolution.
archetypal_resonance: The Vine of the Soul — a bridge between worlds, teacher of the unseen, revealer of truth. Grandmother medicine.
nervous_system_influence: Powerfully activates serotonin system via DMT and MAOIs. Induces altered states lasting 4-6 hours. Intense autonomic activation.
consciousness_interaction: Produces profound alterations in consciousness — visionary states, communication with plant intelligence, access to transpersonal dimensions.
spirit_teaching: Ayahuasca teaches that there are dimensions of reality beyond ordinary perception, and that healing requires facing what we have hidden from ourselves.
integration_body: Rest for days afterward. Nourishing simple foods. Gentle movement. The body processes deeply during and after ceremony.
integration_heart: Emotional material will continue surfacing for weeks. Allow it. Share with trusted integration circle or therapist.
integration_mind: Visions and insights need time to become understanding. Do not rush interpretation. Journal extensively.
integration_spirit: The real ceremony begins when you return to daily life. How will you live differently based on what was shown?
healthy_integration_signs: Lasting behavior change, improved relationships, reduced anxiety/depression, sense of purpose, connection to nature.
incomplete_integration_signs: Ayahuasca tourism (chasing experiences), spiritual bypassing, inability to apply insights, running from ceremony to ceremony.
when_to_seek_support: If experiencing ongoing dissociation, psychotic symptoms, persistent anxiety, inability to return to baseline, or re-traumatization.

use_context_daily: Not for daily use. Sacred ceremonial medicine with deeply specific cultural context.
use_context_practitioner: Not appropriate for clinical herbalism. Sacred sacrament within Native American Church and indigenous Mexican traditions.
use_context_ceremonial: Central sacrament of the Native American Church and Huichol/Wixarika people. All-night prayer ceremony with specific protocol.
use_context_group_vs_private: Always in ceremonial group context with experienced roadman (NAC) or mara-akame (Huichol). Never private recreational use.
cultural_respect_notes: CRITICAL: Peyote is a sacred sacrament for indigenous peoples. Non-Native use is widely considered appropriative. Support indigenous access rather than seeking personal use.
misuse_risks: Recreational use is disrespectful to indigenous traditions and contributes to overharvesting of an endangered cactus. Ecological and cultural harm.
facilitator_qualifications: Native American Church roadman with established authority, or Huichol mara-akame with lineage. No non-indigenous facilitators are appropriate.
facilitator_qualities: Lifelong relationship with peyote medicine, traditional authority, commitment to indigenous community, ceremonial knowledge passed through lineage.
facilitator_red_flags: Any non-indigenous person offering peyote ceremonies, mixing traditions, commercial retreat settings, lack of community accountability.
preparation_framework: Within NAC: prayer, fasting, and following the way of the roadman. Extended preparation within community context.
physiological_contraindications: Nausea is expected. Contraindicated with heart conditions, liver disease, and psychiatric medications.
psychological_considerations: Powerful psychedelic — contraindicated in psychotic disorders. Should only be experienced within cultural container that provides support.
environmental_considerations: Traditional tipi ceremony all night with fire. Very specific ritual setting with deep meaning in every element.
dosage_sensitivity: Determined by roadman within ceremony. Usually 4-12 buttons. Potency varies by cactus age and growing conditions.
interaction_notes: Contains mescaline. Avoid with SSRIs, lithium, heart medications. MAOIs not present but caution with serotonergic drugs.
native_ecosystems: Chihuahuan Desert — southern Texas and northern Mexico. Extremely slow-growing (takes 10-15 years to reach harvestable size).
wildcrafted_vs_cultivated: Wild-harvested traditionally. Cultivation efforts exist but cactus grows extremely slowly. Wild populations are declining.
sustainable_harvesting: CRITICALLY ENDANGERED through overharvesting, habitat loss, and increased non-indigenous demand. Cut above root to allow regrowth. Support conservation.
ethical_sourcing_concerns: MAJOR: Peyote is endangered. Non-indigenous demand threatens indigenous access. Support indigenous land rights and conservation rather than seeking personal supply.
sourcing_standards: Do not source peyote. Support indigenous conservation efforts. If indigenous and NAC member, follow community protocols.
traditional_preparation: Fresh or dried buttons consumed whole or as tea within ceremony. Bitter taste is part of the medicine.
modern_preparation: Should only be prepared traditionally. Synthetic mescaline or San Pedro are alternatives that do not threaten peyote populations.
preparation_potency_notes: Potency varies by age and growing conditions. Older, wild cactus is generally more potent. Dried buttons are concentrated.
intentional_practices: Extended prayer and fasting within NAC tradition. The entire ceremony IS the intentional practice — prayer, song, and communion.
psychospiritual_effects: Profound communion with the divine, heart opening, visionary states, deep prayer states, connection to earth and ancestors.
archetypal_resonance: Grandfather Medicine — ancient, patient, wise. The heart of the earth speaking to those who approach with reverence.
nervous_system_influence: Mescaline activates 5-HT2A receptors. Long duration (8-12 hours). Produces empathogenic and visionary states.
consciousness_interaction: Opens perception to sacred dimensions. Facilitates direct experience of the divine. Heart-centered expansion of awareness.
spirit_teaching: Peyote teaches reverence, patience, and prayer. It is not a drug but a sacrament — a living being offering communion with the Creator.
integration_body: Rest and gentle nourishment after ceremony. The body has been in prayer all night. Honor its need for recovery.
integration_heart: Carry the heart-opening forward into daily relationships. The love experienced in ceremony is real and can be lived.
integration_mind: Insights received in prayer are gifts. Hold them humbly. Share within community, not publicly.
integration_spirit: Live the prayers you made during ceremony. Peyote shows the way — you must walk it.
healthy_integration_signs: Deeper prayer life, commitment to community, humility, service to others, connection to earth.
incomplete_integration_signs: Seeking personal power, appropriating indigenous ceremony, collecting experiences rather than integrating them.
when_to_seek_support: If experiencing spiritual crisis, persistent altered states, or difficulty returning to daily functioning after ceremony.

use_context_daily: Not for daily use. Ceremonial medicine used in Andean traditions. Sessions spaced weeks or months apart.
use_context_practitioner: Used by Andean curanderos for healing ceremonies. Some therapists exploring mescaline-assisted therapy.
use_context_ceremonial: Central to Andean mesa ceremony tradition for 3000+ years. Daytime ceremony unlike most plant medicines. Often at sacred sites.
use_context_group_vs_private: Both. Traditional mesa ceremonies are group events. Private healing sessions with curandero also traditional.
cultural_respect_notes: Sacred medicine of Andean peoples — Peru, Bolivia, Ecuador. Named San Pedro (Saint Peter) for opening the gates of heaven. Honor the Andean lineage.
misuse_risks: Recreational use disconnects the medicine from its healing context. Less endangered than peyote but still deserving of respect.
facilitator_qualifications: Andean curandero with mesa tradition training, or experienced facilitator with deep relationship to the medicine and Andean cosmology.
facilitator_qualities: Connection to Andean tradition, ability to work with daytime energy, mastery of mesa ceremony, genuine care for participants.
facilitator_red_flags: No cultural connection, offering alongside party settings, no screening, mixing with other substances.
preparation_framework: Dietary preparation 1-2 weeks. Light eating day before. Intention setting. Daytime ceremony allows connection to sun and nature.
physiological_contraindications: Contains mescaline — avoid with heart conditions, SSRIs, lithium. Strong nausea and purging is common.
psychological_considerations: Long duration (10-14 hours) requires psychological stability. Not for psychotic spectrum disorders. Emotional content can be intense.
environmental_considerations: Daytime outdoor ceremony is traditional. Connection to sunlight and earth. Safe natural setting with shade available.
dosage_sensitivity: Potency varies greatly by cactus species and preparation. Experienced facilitator determines dosage. Start conservatively.
interaction_notes: Mescaline interacts with SSRIs, lithium, heart medications, and other serotonergic substances.
native_ecosystems: Andes mountains of Peru, Ecuador, Bolivia. Grows at high altitude. Cultivated in gardens and sacred sites.
wildcrafted_vs_cultivated: Widely cultivated — much more sustainable than peyote. Grows faster (harvestable in 3-5 years). Available as ornamental plant.
sustainable_harvesting: Cut columns above base — cactus regenerates. Much more sustainable than peyote due to faster growth and wide cultivation.
ethical_sourcing_concerns: Less urgent than peyote but still deserving of respect. Support Andean communities. Ethical alternative to peyote for mescaline seekers.
sourcing_standards: Cultivated cactus is widely available. Ensure ethical relationship with source. Various Trichocereus species vary in potency.
traditional_preparation: Outer green flesh of cactus boiled for many hours into a thick, bitter liquid. Core and spines removed.
modern_preparation: Traditional boiling method. Some use freeze-dried powder. Tea preparation is most common.
preparation_potency_notes: Outer green layer contains most mescaline. Longer boiling concentrates medicine. Different species (T. pachanoi, T. bridgesii) vary in strength.
intentional_practices: Connect with the sun and earth during ceremony. Set intention for heart opening and clarity. Prayer and gratitude.
psychospiritual_effects: Heart opening, empathy, connection to nature, visual enhancement, sense of cosmic love, clarity about life purpose.
archetypal_resonance: The Heart of the Sun — warm, expansive, loving. Opens the gates between ordinary and sacred reality in daylight.
nervous_system_influence: Mescaline activates serotonin receptors. Long, gentle arc compared to other psychedelics. Empathogenic and visionary.
consciousness_interaction: Gentle expansion of awareness into beauty and interconnection. Heart-centered rather than mind-centered opening.
spirit_teaching: San Pedro teaches that the sacred is not hidden — it is right here in the sunlight, in the earth, in each other. The gates of heaven are always open.
integration_body: Rest the following day. Gentle nourishment. Spend time in nature. The body may feel vibrant and sensitized.
integration_heart: The heart opening is the core gift. Carry compassion and connection forward. Practice loving-kindness.
integration_mind: Clarity about life purpose often emerges. Journal insights and commit to concrete changes.
integration_spirit: San Pedro opens a doorway to lived spiritual experience. Walk through it in daily life, not just in ceremony.
healthy_integration_signs: Increased compassion, clearer life direction, deeper nature connection, improved relationships, sense of beauty.
incomplete_integration_signs: Seeking repeated ceremonies without applying insights, spiritual materialism, neglecting daily practice.
when_to_seek_support: If experiencing prolonged altered states, difficulty with daily functioning, or unresolved emotional material.

use_context_daily: Not for daily use. One of the most powerful plant medicines. Ceremonies are rare, profound events — often once in a lifetime.
use_context_practitioner: Used in clinical settings for addiction interruption (ibogaine). Requires medical monitoring due to cardiac risks.
use_context_ceremonial: Central sacrament of Bwiti tradition in Gabon and Cameroon. Initiation ceremonies are life-defining events lasting 24-72 hours.
use_context_group_vs_private: Traditional Bwiti initiation is communal. Clinical ibogaine treatment is one-on-one with medical staff.
cultural_respect_notes: Sacred plant of the Bwiti people of Central Africa. Iboga shortage threatens indigenous practice. Non-African use should support Bwiti communities.
misuse_risks: LIFE-THREATENING without medical monitoring. Cardiac deaths documented. Never use outside medically supervised or traditional Bwiti context.
facilitator_qualifications: Medical: physician with ibogaine experience, cardiac monitoring capability, emergency resuscitation available. Traditional: initiated Bwiti N'ganga with years of training.
facilitator_qualities: Medical competence AND spiritual awareness. Ability to hold space for extreme psychological material. Calm under pressure.
facilitator_red_flags: No cardiac monitoring, no medical screening, unlicensed operators, mixing with other substances, no emergency protocols.
preparation_framework: Full medical screening including EKG and liver panels. Medication tapering (especially opioids). Psychological preparation. 1-3 day experience with extended aftercare.
physiological_contraindications: STRICT: Heart conditions (QT prolongation risk — potentially fatal). Liver disease. Many medications. Medical screening is non-negotiable.
psychological_considerations: Extremely intense psychological experience. Not for psychotic disorders, severe dissociative disorders, or those without strong psychological foundation.
environmental_considerations: Clinical setting with cardiac monitoring. Or traditional Bwiti temple with experienced community. Emergency medical access essential.
dosage_sensitivity: Flood dose for addiction: 15-25mg/kg ibogaine. Lower doses for psycho-spiritual work. Individual metabolism varies greatly — CYP2D6 polymorphisms affect processing.
interaction_notes: DANGEROUS with opioids (must be cleared first), methadone, cardiac medications, SSRIs, stimulants. Many fatal interactions documented.
native_ecosystems: Tropical rainforests of Central West Africa — Gabon, Cameroon, Republic of Congo.
wildcrafted_vs_cultivated: Primarily wild-harvested. Root bark is used. Cultivation efforts exist but plant is slow-growing.
sustainable_harvesting: ENDANGERED due to increased global demand for ibogaine. Root bark harvest can kill the plant. Reforestation critical.
ethical_sourcing_concerns: CRITICAL: Iboga is being overharvested, threatening both the plant and Bwiti culture. Support conservation. Consider semi-synthetic ibogaine alternatives.
sourcing_standards: Support Bwiti-led conservation. Use Voacanga africana-derived ibogaine (semi-synthetic) to reduce pressure on wild iboga.
traditional_preparation: Root bark scraped, dried, and consumed directly or as tea in Bwiti initiation. Bitter and nauseating.
modern_preparation: Ibogaine HCl (purified alkaloid) in clinical settings. Total alkaloid extract. Root bark. Semi-synthetic from Voacanga.
preparation_potency_notes: Purified ibogaine allows precise dosing. Root bark is less predictable. Total alkaloid extracts retain synergistic compounds.
intentional_practices: Deep intention for healing — often last resort for addiction. Prayer, surrender, willingness to face the deepest self.
psychospiritual_effects: Complete life review, encounter with ancestors, understanding root causes of addiction, ego death, rebirth experience, connection to African spiritual cosmology.
archetypal_resonance: The Judge and the Healer — shows the unvarnished truth of your life, then offers the chance to choose differently.
nervous_system_influence: Affects multiple neurotransmitter systems — serotonin, dopamine, opioid, NMDA. Resets addictive neural pathways. Extended duration (24-36 hours).
consciousness_interaction: Produces a waking dream state with panoramic life review. One of the most powerful consciousness-altering substances known.
spirit_teaching: Iboga teaches that you cannot heal what you refuse to see. It shows the truth — uncomfortable, complete, and ultimately liberating.
integration_body: Extended rest period — days to weeks of fatigue and sensitivity. The body is recalibrating profoundly. Gentle nourishment essential.
integration_heart: Deep emotional processing continues for months. The life review reveals wounds that need tending. Seek ongoing support.
integration_mind: Insights about life patterns and addiction require sustained behavioral change. Understanding alone does not heal.
integration_spirit: Iboga offers a second chance. The spiritual rebirth must be honored through changed living, not just remembered.
healthy_integration_signs: Sustained sobriety, changed relationship patterns, sense of purpose, gratitude, willingness to do ongoing work.
incomplete_integration_signs: Returning to old patterns, not following through on insights, isolating, believing one experience is enough.
when_to_seek_support: Immediately if cardiac symptoms arise. Ongoing addiction support is essential. Therapy for processing life review material.

use_context_daily: Not for daily use. Experiences are brief but extremely intense. Used sparingly and with great intention.
use_context_practitioner: Traditional Mazatec curanderos use in healing ceremonies. Not commonly used in Western clinical settings.
use_context_ceremonial: Sacred plant of the Mazatec people of Oaxaca, Mexico. Used in quiet, dark healing ceremonies by curanderas.
use_context_group_vs_private: Traditional use is private or small group with healer. The quiet, dark setting is essential to the medicine.
cultural_respect_notes: Sacred to the Mazatec people who call it Ska Maria Pastora (Leaves of Mary the Shepherdess). Honor this indigenous lineage.
misuse_risks: Recreational smoking produces terrifying experiences that disrespect the plant. YouTube videos of people smoking salvia are examples of misuse. The plant is gentle when used traditionally.
facilitator_qualifications: Mazatec curandera/o with traditional knowledge, or experienced guide who understands the plant's unique pharmacology and traditional context.
facilitator_qualities: Calm, quiet presence. Ability to hold space in darkness and silence. Understanding that salvia works through surrender, not force.
facilitator_red_flags: Encouraging smoked extracts, party settings, filming experiences, no preparation or integration, treating as entertainment.
preparation_framework: Quiet mind and setting. Darkness or very low light. Fasting for several hours. Set intention clearly. Have a sitter present.
physiological_contraindications: Limited research on physical contraindications. Avoid with unstable medical conditions. Disorientation risk — ensure physical safety.
psychological_considerations: Can produce extreme dissociation and reality distortion. Not for those with dissociative disorders or psychotic vulnerability. Terrifying if unprepared.
environmental_considerations: CRITICAL: quiet, dark, safe environment. Remove hazards. No flames, sharp objects, or stairs nearby. Sitter must be present. No stimulation.
dosage_sensitivity: Fresh leaves (traditional): chew 8-20 leaves. Dried leaf: smoke small amount. Extracts (5x-100x): extremely potent — avoid high concentrations.
interaction_notes: Limited drug interaction data. Unique mechanism (kappa-opioid agonist) — not serotonergic like classic psychedelics. Caution with any CNS-active substances.
native_ecosystems: Cloud forests of Oaxaca, Mexico at 300-1800m elevation. Grows in shaded, moist ravines. Very specific habitat.
wildcrafted_vs_cultivated: Cultivated from cuttings (rarely produces viable seeds). Wild populations are limited and localized in Oaxaca.
sustainable_harvesting: Harvest leaves without damaging the plant. Propagated through cuttings. Wild populations should not be disturbed.
ethical_sourcing_concerns: Respecting Mazatec cultural ownership. Not supporting prohibition that harms indigenous access. Supporting conservation of cloud forest habitat.
sourcing_standards: Live plants from ethical nurseries. Dried leaf from respectful sources. Avoid concentrated extracts — they distort the traditional experience.
traditional_preparation: Fresh leaves chewed in a quid (held in mouth for absorption) in darkness with curandera. Gradual, gentle onset.
modern_preparation: Dried leaf smoked (harsh, intense, brief). Quid of fresh or rehydrated leaves (gentler, longer). Tincture (sublingual). Concentrated extracts (not recommended).
preparation_potency_notes: Traditional chewing produces gentle, extended experience. Smoking produces brief (5-15 min) but overwhelmingly intense experience. Extracts are dangerously potent.
intentional_practices: Sit in darkness. Quiet the mind completely. Ask the plant for guidance. Surrender control — salvia does not respond to willfulness.
psychospiritual_effects: Reality restructuring, merging with objects, dimensional folding, loss of identity, encounter with entities, feeling of being somewhere else entirely.
archetypal_resonance: The Shepherdess — she leads you to unfamiliar dimensions, sometimes terrifyingly, but always returns you. Mary guides through the impossible.
nervous_system_influence: Kappa-opioid receptor agonist — unique among plant medicines. Produces dysphoria rather than euphoria. Powerfully dissociative.
consciousness_interaction: Unlike any other plant medicine — creates complete departure from consensus reality. Merging with surfaces, dimensional shifts, non-human perspectives.
spirit_teaching: Salvia teaches that reality is far stranger than we imagine — that consciousness is not limited to the body or to human experience.
integration_body: Ground yourself physically afterward. Touch the earth. Eat something. Walk. Reconnect with physical reality deliberately.
integration_heart: Salvia can be emotionally disorienting. Allow feelings to settle. The strangeness fades — focus on what remains meaningful.
integration_mind: The experiences may defy language. Do not force interpretation. Some insights emerge weeks later. Draw or paint rather than write.
integration_spirit: Salvia opens a window into the nature of consciousness itself. Sit with the philosophical implications over time.
healthy_integration_signs: Expanded sense of what reality is, humility, philosophical curiosity, respect for consciousness, reduced materialistic certainty.
incomplete_integration_signs: Persistent derealization, existential anxiety, compulsive re-use trying to understand, inability to describe or process experiences.
when_to_seek_support: If experiencing persistent dissociation, derealization, anxiety, or difficulty distinguishing salvia experiences from ordinary reality.

use_context_daily: Not for daily use. Seeds contain LSA (d-lysergic acid amide). Used ceremonially by Mesoamerican cultures.
use_context_practitioner: Not commonly used in clinical herbalism. Traditional use by Zapotec and Aztec healers for divination and healing.
use_context_ceremonial: Called Tlitlitzin by the Aztecs. Used in divination and healing ceremonies in pre-Columbian Mesoamerica.
use_context_group_vs_private: Traditional use was with a healer in private consultation. Quiet, introspective setting.
cultural_respect_notes: Sacred plant of Mesoamerican peoples. Used alongside psilocybin mushrooms and salvia in Aztec and Zapotec tradition. Honor indigenous lineage.
misuse_risks: Commercially treated seeds contain toxic coatings (fungicides). Nausea can be severe. Casual use often leads to unpleasant experiences.
facilitator_qualifications: Traditional Mesoamerican healer or experienced guide familiar with LSA pharmacology and traditional context.
facilitator_qualities: Understanding of the gentle, dreamy quality of LSA. Patience for the extended onset. Comfort with nausea and purging.
facilitator_red_flags: Using commercially treated seeds, dismissing nausea concerns, combining with other substances, no preparation guidance.
preparation_framework: Source untreated organic seeds only. Fast beforehand. Set intention. Quiet, comfortable environment. Have ginger tea for nausea.
physiological_contraindications: Strong vasoconstrictive effects — avoid with cardiovascular conditions. Nausea and cramping are common. Avoid with pregnancy.
psychological_considerations: Milder than LSD but still psychoactive. Not for psychotic spectrum disorders. May produce anxiety in unprepared individuals.
environmental_considerations: Comfortable, quiet indoor setting. Close to bathroom (nausea likely). Warm blankets. Dim lighting. Nature access if feeling well.
dosage_sensitivity: Heavenly Blue: 150-300 seeds (mild to moderate). Onset slow (1-3 hours). Duration 6-10 hours. Individual sensitivity varies greatly.
interaction_notes: LSA is serotonergic — avoid with SSRIs, MAOIs, and other serotonergic substances. Vasoconstrictive — avoid with blood pressure meds.
native_ecosystems: Central America and Mexico. Various Ipomoea species worldwide. Heavenly Blue (I. tricolor) is most commonly used.
wildcrafted_vs_cultivated: Easily cultivated. Grows as an annual vine. Seeds are readily available but must be untreated for safe use.
sustainable_harvesting: Collect seeds after flowers fade and pods dry. Prolific producer. No sustainability concerns with cultivated plants.
ethical_sourcing_concerns: Ensure seeds are organic and untreated. Commercially sold seeds often have toxic fungicide coatings that are dangerous if ingested.
sourcing_standards: Organic, untreated seeds from reputable garden suppliers. Grow your own for certainty. Never use treated commercial seeds.
traditional_preparation: Seeds ground on metate and mixed with water or consumed whole in traditional Mesoamerican practice.
modern_preparation: Seeds eaten whole, ground and soaked in water (cold water extraction), or chewed. Some extract with non-polar/polar solvents.
preparation_potency_notes: Cold water extraction may reduce nausea while preserving LSA. Chewing thoroughly increases absorption. Potency varies by variety and freshness.
intentional_practices: Set clear intention for insight or healing. Create quiet space. Approach with respect for the ancient tradition.
psychospiritual_effects: Dreamy, introspective state, enhanced color and music perception, philosophical insights, gentle visual enhancement, sense of cosmic connection.
archetypal_resonance: The Morning Dreamer — opens consciousness as the flower opens to the sun. Gentle visionary medicine of the dawn.
nervous_system_influence: LSA binds serotonin receptors producing mild psychedelic effects. Gentler than LSD with more sedative, dreamy quality.
consciousness_interaction: Produces a gentle, contemplative altered state — more introspective than visual. A thinking medicine rather than a seeing medicine.
spirit_teaching: Morning Glory teaches that the divine speaks in whispers as much as thunderbolts. Subtle visions carry deep truths.
integration_body: Rest after the experience. Nausea resolves naturally. Gentle food when appetite returns. Stay warm.
integration_heart: Honor the gentle emotional insights that emerge. Morning Glory opens the heart softly.
integration_mind: Journal philosophical insights. The dreamy quality fades but wisdom can be captured in writing.
integration_spirit: Morning Glory connects us to ancient Mesoamerican wisdom traditions. Carry the contemplative quality forward.
healthy_integration_signs: Philosophical depth, appreciation of beauty, gentler perspective on life, contemplative quality.
incomplete_integration_signs: Fixating on nausea rather than insights, dismissing subtle experiences as not strong enough.
when_to_seek_support: If experiencing cardiovascular symptoms, prolonged nausea, or psychological distress lasting beyond the experience.

use_context_daily: Not for daily use. Seeds contain LSA similar to morning glory but more potent. Used sparingly for introspective journeys.
use_context_practitioner: Not used in clinical herbalism. Ethnobotanical use in Hawaiian and Indian traditional contexts.
use_context_ceremonial: Used in traditional Hawaiian and Indian Ayurvedic contexts. Less documented ceremonial use than morning glory.
use_context_group_vs_private: Private, introspective use most appropriate. Quiet settings with trusted companion.
cultural_respect_notes: Native to India (Argyreia nervosa). Naturalized in Hawaii. Traditional use in Indian folk medicine. Respect both cultural contexts.
misuse_risks: Higher potency than morning glory means easier to take too much. Nausea is significant. Commercially treated seeds may be toxic.
facilitator_qualifications: Experienced guide familiar with LSA pharmacology. No formal traditional facilitation lineage widely available.
facilitator_qualities: Patience with extended onset, comfort with nausea, ability to hold calm space for 8-12 hour experience.
facilitator_red_flags: Treating as a legal LSD substitute, ignoring nausea concerns, no preparation guidance, commercially treated seeds.
preparation_framework: Source organic untreated seeds. Fast 6+ hours. Prepare ginger for nausea. Quiet, comfortable setting. Trusted sitter.
physiological_contraindications: Strong vasoconstrictive effects — avoid with cardiovascular conditions and Raynaud's. Nausea common. Avoid in pregnancy.
psychological_considerations: Can produce significant psychedelic effects. Not for psychotic spectrum disorders. May produce anxiety and body load.
environmental_considerations: Comfortable indoor setting with bathroom access. Warm blankets. Low stimulation. Nature access if mobile.
dosage_sensitivity: 4-8 seeds for moderate experience. Individual sensitivity varies widely. Start with 2-3 seeds for first experience. Potency varies by source.
interaction_notes: LSA is serotonergic — avoid with SSRIs and MAOIs. Vasoconstrictive properties interact with cardiovascular medications.
native_ecosystems: India, naturalized throughout tropics including Hawaii. Large woody vine with beautiful flowers.
wildcrafted_vs_cultivated: Cultivated in tropical regions. Seeds available from ethnobotanical suppliers. Can grow in warm climates.
sustainable_harvesting: Vigorous grower. Seeds are abundant. No sustainability concerns with cultivated plants.
ethical_sourcing_concerns: Ensure seeds are untreated and food-grade. Some suppliers apply fungicides. Source from reputable ethnobotanical vendors.
sourcing_standards: Organic, untreated seeds. Hawaiian-grown or Indian-sourced. Verify no chemical treatment. Store cool and dry.
traditional_preparation: Seeds consumed whole or scraped of outer fuzzy coating (which may contribute to nausea) and chewed thoroughly.
modern_preparation: Seeds chewed, cold water extraction, or ground and packed in capsules. Removing outer coating may reduce nausea.
preparation_potency_notes: More potent than morning glory per seed. 4-8 seeds roughly equivalent to 200-400 morning glory seeds. Freshness affects potency.
intentional_practices: Set clear intention. Create comfortable nest. Prepare for extended experience. Have journal ready for insights.
psychospiritual_effects: Dreamy introspection, enhanced pattern recognition, philosophical depth, gentle visual enhancement, connection to inner wisdom.
archetypal_resonance: The Inner Voyager — turns consciousness inward for deep self-reflection and philosophical exploration.
nervous_system_influence: LSA binds serotonin receptors. Produces sedative-psychedelic state. More body load than morning glory.
consciousness_interaction: Deepens introspective capacity. Produces contemplative state with mild visual and cognitive shifts.
spirit_teaching: Hawaiian Baby Woodrose teaches patience with the uncomfortable — that profound insight often arrives alongside physical challenge.
integration_body: Rest fully. Body processing can continue for a day. Gentle food and movement when ready.
integration_heart: Honor emotional insights that arose during the dreamy state. Journal while fresh.
integration_mind: Philosophical insights may be subtle but deep. Review journal entries days later for new understanding.
integration_spirit: Connect insights to daily practice. How does the contemplative depth translate to waking life?
healthy_integration_signs: Deeper self-understanding, philosophical maturity, patience with discomfort, contemplative capacity.
incomplete_integration_signs: Dismissing the experience due to nausea, seeking stronger alternatives, not journaling insights.
when_to_seek_support: If experiencing prolonged cardiovascular symptoms, persistent nausea, or psychological distress beyond the experience window.

use_context_daily: Not for daily use. Complex pharmacology requires careful preparation. Traditional use in specific Siberian shamanic contexts.
use_context_practitioner: Not used in clinical herbalism. Requires specialized knowledge of muscimol/ibotenic acid pharmacology and preparation methods.
use_context_ceremonial: Sacred mushroom of Siberian shamanic traditions (Koryak, Kamchadal peoples). Possibly the Soma of Vedic texts.
use_context_group_vs_private: Traditional Siberian use was shamanistic — the shaman ingested and guided others. Modern use is typically private with experienced guide.
cultural_respect_notes: Deep roots in Siberian, Sami, and possibly ancient Vedic traditions. One of humanity's oldest sacred plants. Approach with historical reverence.
misuse_risks: Raw mushroom contains ibotenic acid (neurotoxic). Improper preparation causes poisoning. Not a recreational substance — requires preparation knowledge.
facilitator_qualifications: Must understand the critical difference between ibotenic acid and muscimol. Experience with proper decarboxylation preparation essential.
facilitator_qualities: Precise knowledge of preparation chemistry, patience for proper processing, respect for the mushroom's power and history.
facilitator_red_flags: Recommending raw consumption, no knowledge of decarboxylation, treating as equivalent to psilocybin, no safety awareness.
preparation_framework: Proper drying and preparation to convert ibotenic acid to muscimol is essential. Start with very small doses. Always have a sitter.
physiological_contraindications: Ibotenic acid is neurotoxic — proper preparation critical. Avoid with liver conditions, kidney disease, and neurological disorders.
psychological_considerations: Produces delirium-like states at higher doses — not the clarity of psilocybin. Dissociative rather than psychedelic. Not for psychiatric vulnerability.
environmental_considerations: Safe indoor environment. Physical containment may be needed at higher doses (sleepwalking behavior reported). Trusted sitter essential.
dosage_sensitivity: Extremely variable between specimens. Properly dried cap: start with 1-3g. Potency varies by season, location, and preparation.
interaction_notes: GABAergic mechanism — avoid with benzodiazepines, alcohol, and sedatives. Do not combine with other psychoactive substances.
native_ecosystems: Birch and pine forests of northern hemisphere — Siberia, Scandinavia, North America, Europe. Mycorrhizal with birch and conifers.
wildcrafted_vs_cultivated: Wild-harvested only — cannot be cultivated as it requires living tree symbiosis. Identification is generally easy but verify carefully.
sustainable_harvesting: Leave some mushrooms for spore dispersal. Harvest mature specimens. Mycelial network is unharmed by fruiting body harvest.
ethical_sourcing_concerns: Identification errors can be dangerous. Ensure positive ID. Respect the forests and tree partnerships that produce these mushrooms.
sourcing_standards: Only use positively identified Amanita muscaria. Proper drying at 170-200F converts ibotenic acid to muscimol. Never consume raw.
traditional_preparation: Dried in sun or over fire (Siberian method). Sometimes consumed via reindeer urine (the reindeer metabolizes ibotenic acid). Dried and smoked.
modern_preparation: Dried at controlled temperature (77-93C) for decarboxylation. Tea from properly dried material. Microdosing protocols emerging.
preparation_potency_notes: CRITICAL: Drying converts neurotoxic ibotenic acid to psychoactive muscimol. Inadequate drying means poisoning. Temperature and time matter.
intentional_practices: Approach with deep respect for one of humanity's oldest sacred plants. Set clear intention. Prepare for a different experience than psilocybin.
psychospiritual_effects: Dreamy, hypnotic state. Altered size perception (macropsia/micropsia). Sedative visions. Contact with ancestral or nature spirits.
archetypal_resonance: The Ancient Shaman's Mushroom — gateway to the spirit world, the red-and-white doorway between worlds. Possibly the original Santa Claus mythos.
nervous_system_influence: Muscimol is a potent GABA-A agonist — sedating and hypnotic. Very different from serotonergic psychedelics. Dream-like rather than visionary.
consciousness_interaction: Produces a hypnagogic, dream-like state of consciousness. Lucid dreaming quality. Different category entirely from classical psychedelics.
spirit_teaching: Fly Agaric teaches that the oldest medicines require the most patience and respect. Ancient does not mean safe without knowledge.
integration_body: Sleep quality may be affected for days. Deep rest needed. Notice dream changes in following nights.
integration_heart: The dream-like visions may carry emotional content. Process gently over days.
integration_mind: Research the mushroom's history and cultural significance. Understanding deepens the experience retroactively.
integration_spirit: Connection to one of humanity's oldest plant relationships. Sit with the enormity of that lineage.
healthy_integration_signs: Deepened respect for ancient traditions, improved dream life, connection to ancestral practices, humility.
incomplete_integration_signs: Cavalier attitude toward preparation, seeking stronger doses, ignoring safety protocols.
when_to_seek_support: If experiencing symptoms of ibotenic acid poisoning (excessive salivation, confusion, agitation), seek immediate medical attention.

use_context_daily: Not for daily use. Contains potent MAOIs (harmine, harmaline). Even small doses require dietary restrictions.
use_context_practitioner: Used in traditional Persian and Middle Eastern medicine. Modern use primarily as an MAOI potentiator for other plant medicines.
use_context_ceremonial: Esfand/Espand — burned as sacred incense in Persian, Turkish, and Central Asian traditions for protection and purification.
use_context_group_vs_private: Incense use is communal (traditional at gatherings). Ingestion should be supervised due to MAOI properties.
cultural_respect_notes: Ancient sacred plant of Persian and Zoroastrian traditions. Called Harmal. Central to Middle Eastern folk medicine and spiritual practice for millennia.
misuse_risks: MAOI properties create dangerous food and drug interactions. Using as a cheap ayahuasca analogue without understanding risks is extremely dangerous.
facilitator_qualifications: Must understand MAOI pharmacology thoroughly. Knowledge of tyramine-free diet. Experience with harmine/harmaline effects.
facilitator_qualities: Precise pharmacological knowledge, understanding of dietary requirements, patience with preparation protocols.
facilitator_red_flags: Recommending Syrian Rue combinations without dietary protocols, no knowledge of MAOI interactions, casual approach to safety.
preparation_framework: Strict tyramine-free diet for 24 hours before and 24 hours after. Full medication review. Small doses to assess sensitivity.
physiological_contraindications: STRICT: Tyramine-rich foods (aged cheese, wine, fermented foods), SSRIs, stimulants, decongestants, many medications. Hypertensive crisis risk.
psychological_considerations: Harmine alone produces introspective, dreamy state. Can intensify emotions. Not for those on psychiatric medications.
environmental_considerations: Quiet setting for ingestion. For incense use, ensure ventilation. Have blood pressure monitoring available for ingestion.
dosage_sensitivity: Seeds: 1-3g for mild MAOI effect. 3-5g for psychoactive effects. Very narrow therapeutic window. Individual variation significant.
interaction_notes: CRITICAL MAOI interactions — potentially fatal with SSRIs, tyramine foods, stimulants, decongestants, opioids, and many medications.
native_ecosystems: Arid regions from Mediterranean to Central Asia. Iran, Turkey, North Africa, Pakistan, India. Dry, disturbed soils.
wildcrafted_vs_cultivated: Both. Grows wild abundantly in native range. Cultivated as ornamental and for seeds. Hardy and drought-tolerant.
sustainable_harvesting: Abundant where native. Prolific seed producer. No sustainability concerns. Considered invasive in some regions.
ethical_sourcing_concerns: Minimal ecological concerns. Cultural respect for Persian and Middle Eastern traditions important.
sourcing_standards: Seeds should be clean, dry, and properly identified. Available from herb suppliers. Organic preferred.
traditional_preparation: Seeds burned as incense (esfand). Seeds ground and consumed for medicinal use. Tea preparation for internal use.
modern_preparation: Seeds ground and encapsulated, tea, smoked, or used in combination with DMT-containing plants (anahuasca).
preparation_potency_notes: MAOI activity begins at low doses. Psychoactive effects require higher doses. Combining with other substances dramatically changes the experience.
intentional_practices: For incense: burn with prayers for protection (Persian tradition). For ingestion: strict preparation, clear intention, respect for the MAOI power.
psychospiritual_effects: Mild: introspection, enhanced dreams. Higher doses: visionary states, contact with ancestral wisdom, emotional depth. As potentiator: amplifies other medicines.
archetypal_resonance: The Protector and Amplifier — guards against evil (incense tradition) and amplifies the power of other medicines (MAOI function).
nervous_system_influence: Reversible MAOI — inhibits monoamine oxidase, increasing serotonin, dopamine, and norepinephrine. Harmine is also mildly psychoactive.
consciousness_interaction: Alone: subtle deepening of awareness, enhanced dreaming. As potentiator: dramatically amplifies consciousness-altering effects of other substances.
spirit_teaching: Syrian Rue teaches about the power of amplification — that small additions can transform experiences. Respect for invisible forces.
integration_body: Maintain dietary restrictions for 24 hours after. Monitor for any adverse physical symptoms. Rest and nourish.
integration_heart: Emotional amplification may continue as MAOI effects persist. Be gentle with yourself.
integration_mind: Journal insights. Reflect on the relationship between amplification and wisdom — more is not always better.
integration_spirit: Connect with the ancient Persian tradition of using this plant for spiritual protection and purification.
healthy_integration_signs: Respectful relationship with potentiating medicines, deepened dream life, connection to Middle Eastern spiritual traditions.
incomplete_integration_signs: Casual approach to MAOI safety, using to potentiate without understanding risks, ignoring dietary protocols.
when_to_seek_support: IMMEDIATELY if experiencing hypertensive crisis symptoms: severe headache, neck stiffness, nausea, rapid heartbeat, chest pain.

use_context_daily: Many use daily but dependence develops with regular use. Dose-dependent effects: stimulant at low doses, opioid-like at high doses.
use_context_practitioner: Not widely used in Western herbalism. Increasingly discussed in harm reduction contexts for opioid withdrawal support.
use_context_ceremonial: Traditional daily use by laborers in Southeast Asia (chewing fresh leaves). Not ceremonial in the sacred sense.
use_context_group_vs_private: Private use is standard. Community support groups important for those using to manage addiction recovery.
cultural_respect_notes: Traditional plant of Thailand, Malaysia, and Indonesia. Used by laborers for energy and pain relief for centuries. Respect Southeast Asian origin.
misuse_risks: Physical dependence with regular use. Withdrawal symptoms similar to opioid withdrawal. Dose escalation over time. Adulterated products on market.
facilitator_qualifications: Addiction medicine specialist or experienced harm reduction counselor for therapeutic use. No facilitation needed for personal use.
facilitator_qualities: Non-judgmental harm reduction approach, understanding of opioid receptor pharmacology, honesty about dependence risks.
facilitator_red_flags: Claiming kratom is completely safe and non-addictive, selling adulterated products, dismissing withdrawal concerns.
preparation_framework: Start with lowest effective dose. Track usage carefully. Schedule regular breaks. Have a plan for tapering if needed.
physiological_contraindications: Liver toxicity reported in some cases. Avoid with liver disease. Respiratory depression at high doses, especially combined with other depressants.
psychological_considerations: Can provide genuine relief for anxiety and depression but dependence complicates this. Withdrawal includes significant mood disturbance.
environmental_considerations: Use in functional daily settings. Not typically impairing at low doses. Higher doses cause sedation — do not drive.
dosage_sensitivity: Low (1-3g): stimulant. Moderate (3-5g): pain relief, relaxation. High (5-8g): strong opioid-like effects. Above 8g: risk of adverse effects.
interaction_notes: Opioid receptor activity — dangerous with other opioids, benzodiazepines, and CNS depressants. May interact with CYP enzymes.
native_ecosystems: Tropical forests of Southeast Asia — Thailand, Malaysia, Indonesia, Papua New Guinea. Requires tropical climate.
wildcrafted_vs_cultivated: Both. Traditional use from wild trees. Increasingly cultivated commercially in Indonesia for export.
sustainable_harvesting: Leaves harvested from mature trees. Trees are not killed. Sustainable when managed responsibly. Monoculture farming expanding.
ethical_sourcing_concerns: Labor conditions in Indonesian kratom farms vary. Quality control is poor in the industry. Heavy metal contamination possible.
sourcing_standards: Lab-tested for alkaloid content, heavy metals, and adulterants. Reputable vendors with third-party testing. AKA GMP certified.
traditional_preparation: Fresh leaves chewed or brewed as tea in Southeast Asia. Workers chew throughout the day for energy.
modern_preparation: Dried powder (toss and wash), capsules, tea, extracts, tinctures. Extracts are much more potent and dependence-forming.
preparation_potency_notes: Strain names (Maeng Da, Bali, etc.) are mostly marketing. Alkaloid content varies by harvest, not name. Extracts concentrate alkaloids dramatically.
intentional_practices: Be honest about why you are using kratom. Track doses and frequency. Schedule breaks. Monitor for tolerance buildup.
psychospiritual_effects: Low doses: increased energy, sociability, optimism. Higher doses: warmth, contentment, pain relief, emotional cushioning.
archetypal_resonance: The Double-Edged Helper — genuinely aids pain and withdrawal but demands respect for its addictive potential.
nervous_system_influence: Mitragynine and 7-hydroxymitragynine act on mu-opioid receptors. Low doses stimulate via adrenergic pathways. Complex pharmacology.
consciousness_interaction: Low doses sharpen focus and energy. Higher doses produce warm, pleasant dreaminess. Does not produce psychedelic states.
spirit_teaching: Kratom teaches about the thin line between medicine and dependence — that what helps can also bind, and awareness is the key.
integration_body: Monitor tolerance and physical dependence honestly. Track dose, frequency, and effects. Schedule regular breaks.
integration_heart: Be honest about emotional dependence vs. genuine therapeutic use. Are you using to feel better or to avoid feeling?
integration_mind: Understand the pharmacology. Knowledge empowers responsible use. Denial of dependence risk is the primary danger.
integration_spirit: Kratom can be a bridge to healing (from opioid addiction) or a new cage. Self-honesty is the spiritual practice.
healthy_integration_signs: Stable low doses, regular breaks, honest self-assessment, maintained social functioning, no dose escalation.
incomplete_integration_signs: Escalating doses, inability to skip days, using to avoid emotions, defensiveness about usage, withdrawal symptoms.
when_to_seek_support: If unable to reduce dose, experiencing withdrawal symptoms, liver pain, or if kratom use is interfering with life functioning.

use_context_daily: Traditional daily use in Pacific Island cultures. Moderate daily use generally well-tolerated. Heavy daily use may affect liver.
use_context_practitioner: Herbalists and naturopaths recommend for anxiety, insomnia, and muscle tension. Clinical evidence supports anxiolytic effects.
use_context_ceremonial: Central to Pacific Island culture — Fiji, Tonga, Samoa, Vanuatu, Hawaii. Shared in formal ceremonies, community gatherings, and conflict resolution.
use_context_group_vs_private: Traditionally communal. Kava ceremonies are social bonding rituals. Private use for anxiety or sleep is also appropriate.
cultural_respect_notes: Sacred plant of Pacific Islander peoples. Kava circles are living traditions. When drinking kava, you are participating in one of the oldest social-plant relationships on earth.
misuse_risks: Heavy chronic use causes kavalactone dermopathy (scaly skin). Liver concerns with poor-quality preparations. Alcohol combination is dangerous.
facilitator_qualifications: No formal facilitation needed. Pacific Island cultural context provides natural facilitation through community.
facilitator_qualities: Understanding of Pacific Island kava culture, ability to create inclusive social space, respect for the slow pace of kava gatherings.
facilitator_red_flags: Combining kava with alcohol, using tudei (two-day) varieties sold as noble kava, aggressive marketing as party drug.
preparation_framework: Traditional preparation involves group ritual. Personal use: mix with water, strain, drink on empty stomach. Start with one shell.
physiological_contraindications: Caution with liver disease. Never combine with alcohol. Avoid with Parkinson medications (dopamine interactions).
psychological_considerations: Anxiolytic without cognitive impairment at moderate doses. Promotes sociability and calm. Reverse tolerance (works better over time).
environmental_considerations: Best in social settings or quiet evening relaxation. Enhances conversation and connection. Do not drive after heavy consumption.
dosage_sensitivity: Traditional shell: 100-250mg kavalactones. 1-3 shells typical. Reverse tolerance means first experiences may be subtle. Consistent use increases sensitivity.
interaction_notes: Avoid with alcohol (liver damage risk). May interact with hepatotoxic drugs, benzodiazepines, and Parkinson medications.
native_ecosystems: Pacific Islands — Vanuatu, Fiji, Tonga, Samoa, Hawaii. Tropical lowland cultivation. Requires warm, moist climate.
wildcrafted_vs_cultivated: Cultivated. Propagated by cuttings (does not produce viable seeds). Different cultivars (chemotypes) have different effects.
sustainable_harvesting: Root harvested after 3-5 years of growth. Plant is destroyed at harvest — sustainability depends on replanting. Support traditional farming.
ethical_sourcing_concerns: Ensure noble kava varieties (not tudei). Support Pacific Island farmers directly. Fair trade practices matter for farming communities.
sourcing_standards: Noble varieties only (Borogu, Melomelo, etc.). Lab-tested for kavalactone content. No tudei or extracted products. Fresh lateral root preferred.
traditional_preparation: Root pounded or chewed, mixed with water, strained through cloth. Communal bowl. Clap before drinking (Fiji tradition).
modern_preparation: Medium-grind root kneaded in water and strained. Instant kava powder. Capsules/extracts (less traditional, more liver concern).
preparation_potency_notes: Traditional water extraction is safest. Solvent extracts (acetone, ethanol) linked to liver concerns. Noble root, traditionally prepared, is the gold standard.
intentional_practices: Drink with gratitude and community awareness. The first shell is for the host, the second for conversation, the third for peace.
psychospiritual_effects: Deep relaxation, enhanced empathy, peaceful sociability, reduction of anxiety and social fear, emotional warmth.
archetypal_resonance: The Peacemaker — brings people together in calm conversation. The anti-alcohol: promotes peace where alcohol promotes aggression.
nervous_system_influence: Kavalactones modulate GABA, dopamine, and sodium channels. Anxiolytic and muscle-relaxing without cognitive impairment.
consciousness_interaction: Subtle enhancement of social awareness and emotional openness. Quiets anxiety without sedation at moderate doses.
spirit_teaching: Kava teaches that peace is communal — that sitting together in calm presence is one of the most healing things humans can do.
integration_body: Notice anxiety reduction in the body. Track sleep improvement. Be aware of skin changes with heavy use.
integration_heart: Allow the emotional warmth to inform sober social interactions. The openness kava fosters can be cultivated naturally.
integration_mind: Notice that anxiety is not a permanent state — kava demonstrates that calm is accessible. Build on this knowledge.
integration_spirit: Community and peace are spiritual values. Kava practice reinforces that healing happens in relationship.
healthy_integration_signs: Reduced anxiety, improved social connection, better sleep, moderate consistent use, no alcohol substitution.
incomplete_integration_signs: Heavy daily use, skin changes, using to avoid rather than engage socially, combining with alcohol.
when_to_seek_support: If experiencing liver symptoms (dark urine, jaundice), skin dermopathy, or if use is escalating to manage increasing anxiety.

use_context_daily: NEVER for daily use. Extremely dangerous tropane alkaloid plant. Narrow margin between psychoactive and lethal dose.
use_context_practitioner: NOT recommended for any internal use by modern practitioners. Historical use only. External use in liniments by experienced herbalists only.
use_context_ceremonial: Used in specific indigenous traditions (Chumash, Aztec, Hindu Shaivite) with extensive cultural knowledge of preparation and dosage. Not for outsiders.
use_context_group_vs_private: Only within specific indigenous ceremonial contexts with experienced elders. NEVER private experimentation.
cultural_respect_notes: Sacred to multiple traditions — Chumash (toloache), Hindu (Shiva's plant), Aztec. These traditions developed over centuries of careful use. Do not attempt to replicate.
misuse_risks: EXTREME DANGER: Unpredictable potency, multi-day delirium, realistic hallucinations (cannot distinguish from reality), hyperthermia, organ failure, death.
facilitator_qualifications: Only indigenous elders with specific lineage knowledge should facilitate. No Western facilitator is qualified for internal datura use.
facilitator_qualities: Lifelong relationship with the plant spanning decades, ability to prepare precise doses, emergency medical knowledge, cultural authority.
facilitator_red_flags: ANY non-indigenous person offering datura ceremonies. Anyone promoting datura as a psychedelic experience. Anyone without decades of lineage training.
preparation_framework: NOT recommended. If encountered in indigenous context: complete trust in elder, full medical disclosure, acceptance of extreme risk.
physiological_contraindications: Heart conditions, glaucoma, urinary retention, liver/kidney disease, hyperthermia risk, ALL medications. Essentially contraindicated for everyone without traditional guidance.
